summary: Verified About section portrait toggle wiring after PNG regeneration. Both /assets/anil_front.png and /assets/anil_side.png are served correctly (HTTP 200, image/png, ~566KB each). Default front image loads (naturalWidth=1024), toggle to side swaps src and loads new image (naturalWidth=1024), toggle back to front works. Active button state correctly highlights the selected pose with the green accent. No console errors during pose toggling. Technical wiring is fully functional — likeness evaluation is deferred to the user per instructions.
critical_code_review_comments: About.jsx uses `key={pose}` on the element which forces React to unmount/remount the image on each toggle — this is intentional and works, but it does mean the browser cache may be re-hit. Acceptable trade-off.
Image `loading="lazy"` is set; since the About section is below the fold, this is appropriate.
context_for_next_testing_agent: About section pose toggle (front/side) is fully wired and working. Files: /app/frontend/public/assets/anil_front.png (566018 bytes) and /app/frontend/public/assets/anil_side.png (566821 bytes). Both served via cloudflare with HTTP 200 + image/png. The component is /app/frontend/src/components/About.jsx with useState('front') and `key={pose}` on the img to force re-render on toggle. data-testids in use: about-section, about-portrait, pose-toggle-front, pose-toggle-side. Visual likeness was NOT evaluated per instructions.
rca of the issue: N/A — this was a verification pass, not a bug-fix RCA. Original user complaint about likeness is a content-quality issue (regenerated PNGs) and must be judged visually by the user; technical delivery of the new assets is confirmed working.
